question.text: For a scheme to be eligible for sale by new cadre distributors, its returns must be equal to or better than the benchmark for how many of the last consecutive years?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** To protect investors, SEBI has stipulated that any scheme sold by new cadre distributors must have delivered returns equal to or better than its benchmark for each of the last 3 consecutive years. This ensures only well-performing schemes are distributed through this channel. **The Trap:** 5 years and 10 years sound more rigorous, but the actual requirement is 3 years. 1 year would be too short to judge consistent performance. **Cross-Exam Context:** This is also tested in Series V-A (MF Distributors) under distribution regulations. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Before recommending a scheme to a client, always check its 3-year track record against its benchmark. This is not just a rule — it is good practice for any distributor.
